title: How to connect Beats headphones to your iPhone
author: Electriz TechTalk
excerpt: Beats headphones and Beats wireless earbuds are made to work brilliantly with iPhones. Since Apple owns Beats, the two are a perfect match - giving...
content:
Beats headphones and Beats wireless earbuds are made to work brilliantly with iPhones. Since Apple owns Beats, the two are a perfect match - giving you quick pairing, a smooth connection, and hassle-free listening.

In this guide, we'll take you through the step-by-step process of connecting your Beats to your iPhone. Whether you're using over-ear headphones like the Beats Studio Pro, or sports earbuds like the Beats Powerbeats Pro 2, everything you need is right here.

## A step-by-step guide to pairing Beats with iPhone

Pairing your Beats is quick and easy once you know the steps:

1. Make sure your iPhone is ready (Bluetooth switched on, iOS updated).
2. Put your Beats into pairing mode.
3. Connect via the pop-up prompt -- or manually if needed.

### 1. Prepare your iPhone

Before pairing, give your iPhone a quick check:

**Update your iOS**: Some features, like instant pop-up pairing, work best on newer iOS versions. Head to **Settings > General > Software Update** to see if there's an update available.

**Switch on Bluetooth**: Open **Control Centre** (swipe down from the top-right on Face ID iPhones, or up from the bottom on older models) and tap the Bluetooth icon. Or go to **Settings > Bluetooth** and toggle it on.

### 2. Put your Beats into pairing mode

Your Beats need to be discoverable before your iPhone can find them -- that's what pairing mode does.

Here's how to enter pairing mode by model:

- **Beats Studio Pro / Solo Pro**. Hold the power button on the right earcup until the LED blinks.
- **Beats Fit Pro / Powerbeats Pro**. With the case lid open, press the case button until the LED flashes white.
- **Beats Flex**. Hold the power button on the left control module.
- **Older Solo/Studio models**. Hold the power button until the indicator light flashes.

### 3. Connect your Beats to iPhone

**Option 1. Automatic pop-up pairing (for W1/H1 chip models)**

- Hold your Beats near your unlocked iPhone.
- A pop-up window should appear with a **Connect** button.
- Tap **Connect**, and you're good to go.

**Option 2. Manual pairing via Bluetooth settings**

If no pop-up shows:

- Go to **Settings > Bluetooth.**
- Wait for your Beats to appear under **Other Devices**.
- Tap on the Beats to connect.

## Troubleshooting common pairing issues

Even though Beats and iPhones are built to work together, connection issues can sometimes still happen. Here's how to fix the most common ones:

- **Beats not showing in Bluetooth list.** Make sure they're in pairing mode (look for the flashing light).
- **Already paired but not reconnecting.** Try toggling Bluetooth off and on again.
- **Still not working?** Restart your iPhone to clear any Bluetooth glitches.
- **Other devices nearby?** Try turning them off temporarily so your iPhone can focus on your Beats.

## How to reset your Beats

If you're still having trouble, a reset can sometimes solve connection problems:

- **Beats Studio Buds / Beats Fit Pro / Powerbeats Pro**. Place the earbuds in the case with the lid open. Hold the system button until the LED flashes red and white.
- **Beats Solo Pro / Solo3 / Studio3**. Hold the power and volume down buttons together for about 10 seconds until the LED blinks.
- **Beats Flex**. Hold the power and volume down buttons for 15 seconds, then release.

## Beats pairing FAQs

### Can I connect my Beats to multiple Apple devices?

Yes. If your Apple devices are signed in with the same Apple ID, your Beats will automatically switch between them.

### Do Beats work with Android phones?

They do. Just use the standard Bluetooth menu to connect. Some iOS-exclusive features, like pop-up pairing, won't be available. But the main functionality works just fine.

### How do I know if my Beats are connected?

You'll hear a short connection sound in your Beats. You can also check in **Control Centre** -- the volume slider will show a headphone icon with "Beats" listed.

### Can I use Siri with my Beats?

Yes, if your Beats model supports it. For example, Powerbeats Pro, Beats Fit Pro, and Solo Pro let you activate Siri using the "Hey Siri" command.
